pending.body: High: final_review still bypasses the new per-member retry path during engine-side reviewer probing. In src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:9487-9512, an optional reviewer that hits a transient check_availability error is dropped from the panel immediately, before execute_final_review_panel/invoke_final_review_member can apply the new retry loop. If that transient drop takes the panel below quorum, src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:9524-9544 returns AppError::InsufficientPanelMembers, but that error is non-retryable (src/shared/error.rs:345-354; src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5842-5863), so a single transient optional-reviewer probe failure can still hard-fail final_review. Even when quorum survives, the stage now votes with a smaller panel than configured, which can change consensus from a temporary backend blip. This needs to use the same transient classification/retry path as actual reviewer invocations instead of treating transient probe failures as durable panel shrinkage.

Medium: resume still treats transient final_review backend probe failures as stage drift. In src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2250-2287, resume re-checks the arbiter and required reviewers with adapter.check_availability and converts any non-exhaustion failure into ResumeDriftFailure. That means a temporary network/probe blip during `run resume` prevents the failed final_review stage from resuming at all, even though the invocation path in src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs would now retry the same backend once execution starts. Optional reviewers are also silently dropped on resume by src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2265-2299 for non-exhaustion probe errors, so resume can change panel membership for transient reasons.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 8 -> Plan and Implement
